findings.evidence: `rg` hits: `code/TauCeti/FieldTheory/SquareClassGroup.lean:35: abbrev SquareClassGroup ... := Additive Kˣ ⧸ (Subgroup.square Kˣ).toAddSubgroup` and `code/TauCeti/Algebra/Group/ElementaryTwoQuotient.lean:52: abbrev elementaryTwoQuotient ... := ModN (Additive G) 2`; PR docstring also states this is the same construction for `G = Kˣ`.
findings.fix: Either redefine the existing `SquareClassGroup` API through `elementaryTwoQuotient`, or add a formal equivalence/bridge showing `SquareClassGroup K ≃ₗ[ZMod 2] elementaryTwoQuotient Kˣ` and relating `squareClass` to `elementaryTwoQuotientMk`.
findings.issue: `elementaryTwoQuotient (Kˣ)` is the same square-class quotient already exposed as `TauCeti.SquareClassGroup K`, but the PR adds no formal equivalence or compatibility lemmas, creating parallel APIs for the same construction.

summary: The new general quotient reuses Mathlib `ModN`, but it introduces a second formal square-class API for the existing `Kˣ/(Kˣ)²` construction without a bridge. That leaves users with parallel incompatible names for the same object.
